title: Housing for an automotive lighting device, method for manufacturing such a housing and automotive lighting device
abstract: The invention provides a housing (1) for an automotive lighting device, the housing (1) having a demoulding direction (d1), defined as the direction along which the housing is configured to be introduced and extracted from a mould, wherein the housing comprises a wall (2) which forms an angle between 1ֳ‚ֲ÷ and 25ֳ‚ֲ÷ with respect 5 to the demoulding direction (d1) and an angle between 1ֳ‚ֲ÷ and 25ֳ‚ֲ÷ with respect to a second direction (d2), the second direction (d2) being perpendicular to the demoulding direction (d1).
claims:
Claims Revendications [Revendication 1] Housing (1) for an automotive lighting device, the housing (1) having a demoulding direction (d1), defined as the direction along which the housing is configured to be introduced and extracted from a mould, wherein the housing comprises a wall (2) which forms an angle between 1ֳ‚ֲ° and 25ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to the demoulding direction (d1) and an angle between 1ֳ‚ֲ° and 25ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to a second direction (d2), the second direction (d2) being perpendicular to the demoulding direction (d1). [Revendication 2] Housing according to claim 1 , wherein the wall (2) forms an angle between 8ֳ‚ֲ° and 20ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to the demoulding direction (d1) and an angle between 8ֳ‚ֲ° and 20ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to the second direction (d2). [Revendication 3] Housing according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the wall (2) comprises a hydrophobic substance, with a Young contact angle higher than 80ֳ‚ֲ° [Revendication 4] Housing according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the hydrophobic substance comprises a fluorinated based polymer. [Revendication 5] Housing according to claim 4, wherein the hydrophobic substance comprises at least one of PTFE, fluorosiloxanes or organic siloxanes. [Revendication 6] Housing according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the wall comprises at least one sink hole (3) suitable to allow water to exit the housing. [Revendication 7] Housing according to claim 6, wherein the sink hole (3) is covered by a ventilation cap. [Revendication 8] Housing according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the wall (1) comprises a groove (4). [Revendication 9] Housing according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the wall (1) is located in a position intended to be the lowest part of the housing when installed in an automotive vehicle. [Revendication 10] Method for manufacturing a housing according to any of the preceding claims, comprising the steps of: 

 - demoulding a housing structure (1 ֳ¢ֲ€ֲ™) comprising a wall (2) which forms an angle between 1ֳ‚ֲ° and 25ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to the demoulding direction (d1 ) and an angle between 1 0 and 25ֳ‚ֲ° with respect to a second direction (d2), the second direction (d2) being perpendicular to the demoulding direction (d1); - depositing a fluorinated based polymer, such as PTFE, fluorosiloxanes or organic siloxanes, on the wall (2). [Revendication 11] Method according to claim 10, further comprising the step of masking a glue channel of the housing structure to prevent the deposition of the fluorinated based polymer. [Revendication 12] Method according to any of claims 10 or 11 , wherein the deposition of the fluorinated based polymer is done by one of these methods - directly by physical vapour deposition of the fluorinated based polymer, such as by radiofrequency magnetron sputtering; or - by plasma-enhanced chemical vapour deposition of the fluorinated based polymer; or - by sol-gel coating of the fluorinated based polymer. [Revendication 13] Method according to claim 12, wherein the step of depositing the fluorinated based polymer further comprises the use of a highly organics layer, such as organosiloxane or hydrocarbide precursors. [Revendication 14] Method according to any of claims 12 or 13, further comprising the step of performing a plasma treatment on the surface of the wall before depositing the fluorinated based polymer. [Revendication 15] Automotive lighting device (10) comprising a housing according to any of claims 1 to 9 and a plurality of solid-state light sources (5).
